Author: Wan C (2015)
Qualifications: Fractionation was combined with mass spectrometry from five diverse animal species to predict co-complex protein interactions conserved across metazoa using an integrative computational scoring procedure along with an SVM approach. The significant data set of 16655 PPI, was derived from a set of more than 1M interactions by examining a ROC curve of predicted interactions against reference annotated complexes at a 67.5% cumulative precision.

Author: Wan C (2015)
Qualifications: Fractionation was combined with mass spectrometry from five diverse animal species to predict co-complex protein interactions conserved across metazoa using an integrative computational scoring procedure along with an SVM approach. The significant data set of 16655 PPI, was derived from a set of more than 1M interactions by examining a ROC curve of predicted interactions against reference annotated complexes at a 67.5% cumulative precision.

Author: Nassa G (2019)
Qualifications: interaction detected using the TAP (tandem affinity purification) procedure followed by LC-MS/MS (liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry) to identify ER-alpha-interacting proteins; this interaction was detected in at least 2 of 3 samples not treated with, as well as in at least 2 of 3 samples pre-treated with, RNase, suggesting that the interaction can occur in the absence or presence of RNA; this interaction was considered statistically significant because (a) a p-value of p<0.05 was obtained using the Mascot software and (b) a high or medium protein FDR (false discovery rate) confidence level was reported using the ProteomeDiscoverer software

Author: Nassa G (2019)

Author: Li Y (2020)
Qualifications: KDF1 was tandem affinity purified from differentiated keratinocytes labeled in a SILAC (stable isotope labeling by amino acids in cell culture) experiment in which cells containing tagged target were labeled with heavy isotope and control (untagged) cells were labeled with light isotope and associated proteins were identified by mass spectrometry in two separate experiments; a protein was considered a valid interactor in either experiment if it had a log2 normalized high-to-low ratio of greater than 1 or if only heavy-labeled protein was identified; a protein was considered an interactor if the interaction was valid in one experiment and either valid or not observed in the other experiment. A numerical score indicates the maximum of one or both log2 normalized high-to-low ratios. If an interaction was observed in heavy-only cells in one experiment and either similarly observed or not observed in the other experiment, then that interaction was loaded into the BioGRID database without a score.
